# PRG-11 Architecture Clean-Out — Moot-by-Refactor Scan

**Date:** 2026-06-11
**Scope:** Identity Foundation (IF) PRs 817, 818, 845, 855, 859, 860, 867, 874, 875, 876, 882, 888, 897, 902, 903 merged to main.
**Method:** For each PRG-11 in-scope finding, locate the flagged code surface in the current tree, cross-reference with the IF file-touch union, and verify in-place. File-touch set alone is insufficient — touched files can still contain the issue; untouched files can be mooted by caller rewrites. All verdicts carry `file:line` evidence.
**Status:** Generated by read-only scan agent; no Cosmo writes; informational input to PRG-11 activation.

---

## IF File-Touch Set (union of all 15 PRs)

Key surfaces relevant to PRG-11 findings:

- `apps/api/src/services/consent.ts` (PR 855, 859, 888)
- `apps/api/src/services/notifications.ts` + `notifications/email.ts` (PR 859)
- `apps/api/src/services/policy-engine/` (new, PR 860)
- `apps/api/src/services/session/session-exchange-router.ts` + `session-exchange-spine.ts` (new, PR 860)
- `apps/api/src/services/session/session-exchange.ts` (PR 860)
- `apps/api/src/services/llm/router.ts` (PR 855)
- `apps/api/src/services/family-access.ts` (PR 859)
- `apps/api/src/services/billing/tier.ts` + `revenuecat.ts` (PR 876, 897)
- `apps/api/src/middleware/metering.ts` (PR 882, 903)
- `apps/api/src/middleware/auth.ts` (PR 875)
- `apps/mobile/src/hooks/use-consent.ts` (PR 875)
- `apps/api/src/inngest/functions/freeform-filing.ts`, `monthly-report-cron.ts` (PR 818)
- `apps/api/src/services/pii-scrub.ts`, `learner-profile.ts`, `evaluate.ts` (PR 902)
- `apps/api/src/inngest/registration-sync.guard.test.ts` (PR 867)

## Per-Finding Detail

### Theme 1 — Circular Dependencies

#### F-011 — LIVE: `curriculum.ts` ⇄ `language-curriculum.ts` runtime cycle

Both directions confirmed in current tree:
- `apps/api/src/services/curriculum.ts:1` — `import { regenerateLanguageCurriculum } from './language-curriculum'`
- `apps/api/src/services/language-curriculum.ts:9` — `import { ensureDefaultBook } from './curriculum'`

Neither file appears in the IF file-touch set. The cycle is a runtime (not type-only) bidirectional dependency. The `regenerateLanguageCurriculum` call in `curriculum.ts` is the back-dispatch smell; the `ensureDefaultBook` call in `language-curriculum.ts` is the forward dependency. Neither was refactored by the IF work.

#### F-029 — MOOT: `consent.ts` ⇄ `notifications.ts` cycle broken by PR 859

PR 859 introduced `apps/api/src/services/notifications/email.ts` and moved email primitives there. `consent.ts` now imports `sendEmail`, `formatConsentRequestEmail`, `EmailOptions` from `notifications/email.ts` — not from `notifications.ts`. The reverse edge (`notifications.ts` → `consent.ts`) still exists (`isGdprProcessingAllowed` at line 22 of `notifications.ts`), but it is now one-directional. The cycle (A→B and B→A) is gone.

Evidence: `consent.ts:33` — `from './notifications/email'`; `notifications.ts:22` — `import { isGdprProcessingAllowed } from './consent'`; no import of `notifications.ts` in `consent.ts`.

#### F-030 — LIVE: Type-only cycle `exchanges.ts` ⇄ `exchange-prompts.ts`

Both directions still present (one is a runtime import):
- `apps/api/src/services/exchanges.ts:41` — runtime import of multiple symbols from `./exchange-prompts`; `exchanges.ts:546` re-exports `buildSystemPrompt` from `./exchange-prompts`
- `apps/api/src/services/exchange-prompts.ts` — `import type { ExchangeContext } from './exchanges'`

The runtime direction (`exchanges.ts` → `exchange-prompts`) and type-only direction (`exchange-prompts` → `exchanges`) form a mutual dependency. Neither file is in the IF touch set. Note: the type-only direction is compile-erased; the `exchanges.ts` → `exchange-prompts` direction is runtime.

#### F-034 — LIVE: Type-only layer inversions (services into middleware)

Both API-side inversions still present:
- `apps/api/src/services/family-access.ts:13` — `import type { ProfileMeta } from '../middleware/profile-scope'` (PR 859 touched this file but did not move the import)
- `apps/api/src/services/quiz/orchestrate-round.ts:14` — `import type { ProfileMeta } from '../../middleware/profile-scope'` (PR 875 touched this file but did not move the import)

Both are type-only (`import type`) — erased at compile time, no runtime coupling. The `ProfileMeta` type needs to be moved to a shared services/types module to clean the direction. Not addressed by IF work.

### Theme 2 — God Modules / Oversized Files

#### F-007 — LIVE: Oversized service verticals

Current sizes vs. audit sizes:

| File | Audit LOC | Current LOC | Change |
|---|---|---|---|
| `services/session/session-exchange.ts` | 3,321 | 3,266 | -55 (PR 860 carved out `session-exchange-router.ts` 110 LOC + `session-exchange-spine.ts` 44 LOC) |
| `services/session/session-crud.ts` | 2,228 | 2,249 | +21 |
| `services/curriculum.ts` | 2,643 | 2,718 | +75 |
| `services/learner-profile.ts` | ~1,948 | 1,950 | +2 |

`session-exchange.ts` shed ~55 lines via the new router/spine modules but remains a 3,266-line monolith with no meaningful seam extractions. Mobile god screens (`shelf/[subjectId]/book/[bookId].tsx`, `homework/camera.tsx`, `sign-in.tsx`, `session-summary/[sessionId].tsx`) were not in IF scope and are unchanged. F-007 LIVE; the IF work is a marginal improvement, not a fix.

#### F-014 — LIVE: `test-seed.ts` 5,668 LOC + production-bundle risk

`apps/api/src/services/test-seed.ts` is now 5,772 LOC (grew by 104 lines). Still imported by `apps/api/src/routes/test-seed.ts`, maintaining the production-bundle risk. Not in IF touch set.

#### F-031 — LIVE: Navigation and conflict hotspot files grew

Several files flagged in F-031 were touched by IF and grew:
- `apps/api/src/services/llm/router.ts`: 1,463 → 1,732 LOC (PR 855 added significant routing logic)
- `apps/api/src/services/consent.ts`: 1,260 → 1,352 LOC (PRs 855, 859, 888)
- `apps/api/src/routes/sessions.ts`: 1,612 → 1,665 LOC (PR 882)

The IF work was correctness work, not decomposition. The oversized-files class remains and some hotspots are larger.

### Theme 3 — Domain Organisation

#### F-009 — LIVE: `metering.ts` filename collision

Both files exist in current tree:
- `apps/api/src/services/metering.ts` (quota math — top-level)
- `apps/api/src/services/billing/metering.ts` (DB mutators — billing subdomain)

Neither was renamed by IF work. PR 882 and 903 touched `middleware/metering.ts` (unrelated file — the metering middleware) but not the two service-layer `metering.ts` files.

#### F-010 — MOOT: Billing domain half-migrated

`apps/api/src/services/billing.ts` is now a pure re-export facade (confirmed: file header states "This file is now a re-export facade. All logic lives in billing/ sub-modules.") and re-exports from `billing/types`, `billing/subscription-core`, `billing/tier`, `billing/top-up`, etc. The four flat files that were "never moved" have been absorbed into the `billing/` subdomain. PRs 876 and 897 were part of this migration. MOOT.

#### F-103 — PARTIAL: Challenge Round mastery smeared — consolidation started but not connected

`apps/api/src/services/challenge-round/persistence.ts` (new file, not explicitly in IF PR file-touch set but present in current tree) exports `persistChallengeRoundMasteryEvidence` at line 135. However, `apps/api/src/services/session/session-exchange.ts:572` still defines its own private local `persistChallengeRoundMasteryEvidence` function and calls it internally at lines 699, 810, 857. The new `persistence.ts` export is only exercised by its own test file — the main flow (`session-exchange.ts`) does not import from `challenge-round/persistence.ts`. Additionally, `finalizeChallengeRoundIfReady` (the outer orchestrator) remains in `session-exchange.ts:683`. The mastery knowledge is now split across five locations rather than four: `challenge-round/evaluation.ts`, `challenge-round/state.ts`, `challenge-round/persistence.ts` (new, unused by main flow), `session-exchange.ts` (private `persistChallengeRoundMasteryEvidence`), and `session-exchange.ts` (`finalizeChallengeRoundIfReady`).

#### F-104 — LIVE: `session.completed` dispatch stranded in route

`apps/api/src/routes/sessions.ts:1588` — `dispatchSessionCompletedEvent` is still a 70-line private async function in the route module. It is called from three separate handlers at lines 1287, 1422, and 1470. PR 882 touched `sessions.ts` but only added metering-side changes. The dispatch remains in the route, not promoted to the service layer.

#### F-105 — LIVE: Retry-filing duplication; cap drifted

Two handlers still implement the retry flow independently:
- `apps/api/src/routes/sessions.ts:330` — uses `FILING_CONFIG.maxRetries` (dynamic)
- `apps/api/src/routes/filing.ts:97` — hardcodes `>= 3` (literal, already drifted)

Neither file was in the IF touch set. The live bug (cap divergence) persists.

### Theme 4 — Test Coverage Gaps (Architecture Class)

#### F-097 — LIVE: IDOR check in `orchestrate-round.ts` has no regression test

PR 875 touched `apps/api/src/services/quiz/orchestrate-round.test.ts` but the IDOR ownership check security guard remains untested. The `[SECURITY]`-tagged ownership validation in `orchestrate-round.ts` has no negative-path break test. A future edit could silently remove it.

#### F-098 — LIVE: `isClosePathAutoFileEligible` guard has no test

`apps/api/src/services/session/session-filing-dispatch.ts:12` — function exists; no co-located `session-filing-dispatch.test.ts` file; grep across all `*.test.ts` files finds zero coverage of this guard. Not in IF touch set.

#### F-099 — LIVE: Retention cutoff math in `webhook-idempotency-purge.ts` has no test

`apps/api/src/inngest/functions/webhook-idempotency-purge.ts` — no `*.test.ts` companion. Not in IF touch set.

#### F-156 — LIVE: GC1 mock guard misses multiline `jest.mock` calls

`scripts/check-gc1-pattern-a.ts:35` — `MOCK_LINE = /jest\.(?:mock|doMock)\(\s*['"\`](\.\.?\/[^'"\`]+)['"\`]/` requires the path specifier on the SAME line as `jest.mock(`. A two-line form:
```
jest.mock(
  './some-path',
```
does not trigger `MOCK_LINE.test(content)` because line 1 (`jest.mock(`) has no path. The bypass was not addressed in any IF PR.

#### INV-2 — LIVE: Internal `jest.mock()` backlog (GC6 sweep class)

Pre-IF survey (workflow-2 audit, 2026-05-29): 153 genuine unescaped internal violations — API 103, mobile 50. IF touched several test files (`metering.test.ts`, `auth.test.ts`, `consent.test.ts`, `sessions.test.ts`) but added new mocks under `gc1-allow` annotations rather than burning down legacy violations. The GC6 natural burn-down (touching a test file requires converting its mocks) would have reduced the count only for those specific files. Net reduction is small; the backlog remains ~150 sites.

### Theme 5 — Mobile Navigation / UX Resilience

#### F-108 — LIVE: V0/V1 entry-gating copy-pasted across 8 screen layouts

Pattern `const blocked = FEATURE_FLAGS.MODE_NAV_V1_ENABLED ? !navigationContract.canEnter('…') : navigationContract.isParentProxy` still present at:
- `apps/mobile/src/app/(app)/session/_layout.tsx:17`
- `apps/mobile/src/app/(app)/quiz/_layout.tsx:121`
- `apps/mobile/src/app/(app)/dictation/_layout.tsx:67`
- `apps/mobile/src/app/(app)/homework/_layout.tsx:12`
- `apps/mobile/src/app/(app)/mentor-memory.tsx:248`
- `apps/mobile/src/app/(app)/topic/relearn.tsx:374`
- `apps/mobile/src/app/(app)/practice/index.tsx:444`
- `apps/mobile/src/app/(app)/progress/index.tsx:76,79,82,331,492` (multiple triples)

Comments in several files explain the V0 fallback rationale, but the deduplication into `canEnter()` was not done. None of these screens are in the IF file-touch set.

#### F-109 — LIVE: Home surface routing via magic prop

`apps/mobile/src/app/(app)/home.tsx:167` — passes `showParentHome={false}` to `<LearnerScreen>`. `apps/mobile/src/components/home/LearnerScreen.tsx:492` — still contains `if (showParentHome && navigationContract.gates.showFamilyHome) { return <ParentHomeScreen> }`. `apps/mobile/src/app/(app)/own-learning.tsx:42` — also passes `showParentHome={false}`. The magic prop pattern is unchanged; neither file is in the IF touch set.

#### F-111 — LIVE: Quota-refund policy in SSE route in five places

`apps/api/src/routes/sessions.ts:566,846,948,1070` (and line 570 context) — `safeRefundQuota` called inline in four separate error paths, each re-assembling `quotaDecrementSource`/`quotaModel`/`topUpCreditId`. PR 882 touched `routes/sessions.ts` but only for metering middleware changes.

### Theme 6 — Data Access / Profile Context

#### F-106 — LIVE: Profile-context resolution ~20 times

43 `parseConversationLanguage` call sites remain across production source (grep count, excluding tests). Representative sites after IF work:
- `apps/api/src/inngest/functions/monthly-report-cron.ts:441,559,566,614,625` (this file was touched in PR 818 — the profile reads remain inline)
- `apps/api/src/inngest/functions/session-completed.ts:1033,1191,1274,1707`
- `apps/api/src/routes/sessions.ts:1457,1534`

No `getProfileContext(db, profileId)` consolidation function was created. `apps/api/src/services/profile.ts` (touched in PR 855) still exposes `loadProfileRowById` but not the proposed unified context helper. The 20× leaky seam is unchanged.

#### F-107 — LIVE: `loadTopicTitle` defined twice with divergent ownership joins

- `apps/api/src/inngest/functions/session-completed.ts:90` — 3-table join via `subjects.profileId` (lighter, potentially leaky)
- `apps/api/src/services/assessments.ts:754` — delegates to canonical `findOwnedCurriculumTopic` 4-table join in `services/curriculum-topic-ownership.ts`

`session-completed.ts` was not in the IF touch set. The safer canonical version in `assessments.ts` remains correct; the duplicate in `session-completed.ts` remains a cross-profile leak risk.

#### F-112 — LIVE: Two data-access adapters for one concern

`apps/api/src/services/session/session-topic.ts:19` still carries the parent-chain pattern comment. Four Inngest functions still carry "rules do NOT apply here" exception comments. The `createScopedRepository` vs parent-chain split is unchanged by IF work.

---

### Theme 7 — Correctness Races

#### F-169 — LIVE: SM-2 lost-update race in `reviewVocabulary`

`apps/api/src/services/vocabulary.ts:274` — the comment reads "Wrap read-compute-write in a transaction to prevent SM-2 race conditions." However, `ensureVocabularyRetentionCard` performs a SELECT inside the transaction without `.for('update')`. At READ COMMITTED isolation (Neon default), two concurrent transactions can both read the same card state, both compute divergent SM-2 parameters, and the second write silently overwrites the first. No `SELECT FOR UPDATE` / `.for('update')` call found in `vocabulary.ts`. File not in IF touch set.

#### F-170 — LIVE: Pending celebration writes lose concurrent updates

`apps/api/src/services/celebrations.ts:84` — `findHomeSurfaceCache(db, profileId)` executes before the transaction begins at line 116. Two concurrent callers both read the same pending list, both compute a new list, and both write it — one write wins, one celebration is lost. The SELECT FOR UPDATE inside `writeHomeSurfacePendingCelebrations` serialises the DB writes but does not protect against the pre-read stale state. `celebrations.ts` is not in the IF touch set.

#### F-171 — LIVE: Celebration read happens outside SELECT FOR UPDATE lock

`apps/api/src/services/celebrations.ts:84` — `findHomeSurfaceCache` is the read; `db.transaction(...)` opens at line 116. The decision logic (`hasDuplicate` check, new entry construction) happens with pre-lock data. The `home-surface-cache.ts:208` `.for('update')` is acquired inside the transaction during the write, but the celebration dedup calculation is already done with potentially stale data. `celebrations.ts` and `home-surface-cache.ts` are not in the IF touch set.

---

### Theme 8 — Doc-Rot

#### F-012 — LIVE: `architecture.md` stale circular-dep warning

`docs/architecture.md:765` — "An agent importing a Drizzle type into a shared schema creates a circular dependency — the schema package must remain a leaf."
`docs/architecture.md:896` — "schemas never imports from database. Circular dependencies are build-breaking errors."

Both statements are correct as preventive guidance, but the L-gap-delta audit identified them as describing a "non-existent" database→schemas edge as if it were a confirmed current risk. The file was not modified by any IF PR. The finding is LOW severity (preventive doc that could mislead agents checking for cycles that do not exist).

---

### Special Cases — MOOT by IF Work

#### F-029 — MOOT (confirmed above)
`apps/api/src/services/notifications/email.ts` was introduced by PR 859, breaking the bilateral cycle between `consent.ts` and `notifications.ts`.

#### F-010 — MOOT (confirmed above)
`billing.ts` converted to a pure facade via PRs 876, 897.

#### F-153 — MOOT
PR 875 resolved the divergent `useRestoreConsent` duplication. `apps/mobile/src/hooks/use-restore-consent.ts` is now the single canonical hook. `apps/mobile/src/hooks/use-consent.ts:241` carries a tombstone comment `// [F-153] useRestoreConsent was duplicated here…`. Both callers (`child/[profileId]/index.tsx`, `components/family/WithdrawalCountdownBanner.tsx`) use the canonical import.

| **LIVE** | 23 | F-007, F-009, F-011, F-012, F-014, F-030, F-031, F-034, F-097, F-098, F-099, F-104, F-105, F-106, F-107, F-108, F-109, F-111, F-112, F-156, F-169, F-170, F-171 |

**Total scanned:** 28 active findings + 1 sweep class = 29 items. 2 excluded-deferred not scanned.

### Implication for PRG-11 Scoping

The Identity Foundation rewrite produced 3 clean moots (F-029 cycle broken, F-010 billing migrated, F-153 duplicate hook removed) and 1 partial start on F-103 (challenge-round persistence module created but not wired into the main flow). The remaining 23 LIVE findings are structurally untouched: the circular dependencies (F-011, F-030), the god modules (F-007, F-014, F-031), the domain-organisation issues (F-009, F-104, F-105), the nav/UX copy-paste (F-108, F-109, F-111), the data-access patterns (F-106, F-107, F-112), the test coverage gaps (F-097, F-098, F-099, F-156, INV-2), and the correctness races (F-169, F-170, F-171) are all intact. The charter's moot-risk candidates (F-011, F-031, F-106, F-107, F-108, F-109, F-112) are all LIVE — the IF rewrite touched the session-exchange and policy-engine modules but did not address the architectural patterns in those surrounding files. PRG-11's scope is essentially unchanged from the pre-IF estimate of 28 active items; drop 3 moots and 1 partial, net active workload ~24 findings (+ INV-2 sweep class) entering activation.
